title: identity — product analytics
description: Product-analytics events emitted for the identity substrate. These are DISTINCT from OpenTelemetry metrics: analytics feed product+growth dashboards (segmented by identity fingerprint under 'functional' consent tier), while metrics feed operational dashboards (aggregate-only, no fingerprint). No PII on any event.

events.name: Identity Registered
events.description: New identity created. registration_source = self_signup|invitation|admin_provisioned|sso_first_login. Essential-tier because it's a transactional-milestone event (funnel analytics).

events.name: Email Verified
events.description: Identity holder confirmed email ownership. elapsed_since_registration_hours drives the activation-funnel dashboard.

events.name: MFA Enrolled
events.description: Identity holder completed TOTP enrolment. Drives the MFA-adoption dashboard.

events.name: Recovery Codes Regenerated
events.description: Rare event. Signals user lost access to primary MFA device or is proactively rotating.

events.name: Identity Erasure Requested
events.description: GDPR Art. 17 erasure request received. reason_category = user_initiated|compliance_officer|end_of_retention. Essential-tier because compliance reporting relies on the count regardless of user consent state.

notes: `identity_fingerprint` is a SHA-256 of the identity_id, NOT the identity_id itself. Segments the event by identity without exposing the underlying ULID to downstream analytics tooling.

None of these events carry email, dob, IP, or user-agent. If a downstream dashboard needs geolocation for signup analysis, resolve country_code at the collector layer (before persisting) and pass ONLY the country_code.
